type.description: Approver identity type: github-team | ado-group | user

value.description: Approver identifier — team slug, group name, or user address

CommonLifecycleModel.description:
Lifecycle phases for common models.
Maps phase names to phase configurations.
Phase names follow pattern: {command}_{action}_{suffix}
Examples: config_clear, config_fetch, deploy_plan_before, deploy_apply_after
CommonLifecycleModel.title: CommonLifecycleModel

CommonLifecyclePhaseModel.description: Model for common lifecycle phase with script validation.
CommonLifecyclePhaseModel.title: CommonLifecyclePhaseModel

DeploymentApprovalModel.description:
Deployment-level approval metadata.

Presence of this block signals that approvals are declared for this deployment.
An empty ``approvers`` dict is silently treated as no gate.
The CLI emits this metadata for audit; enforcement is done by the CI/CD system.

approvers.description: Named approver entries. Key is a short identifier used as a cross-reference from stage overrides.
approvers.title: Approvers

DeploymentApprovalModel.title: DeploymentApprovalModel

file.description: Path to the file (resolved and validated at load time)
file.title: File

description.description: Optional description of the referenced file
description.title: Description

tags.description: Optional tags (list of values for categorization)
tags.title: Tags

DeploymentSpecModel.description: Model for deployment specification (properties, workspace, stages).

properties.description: Deployment-specific properties and configurations. Validated against configuration.spec.deployment.properties schema if defined.
properties.title: Properties

layers.description: Deployment layer values (keys must match configuration.spec.layering[].name). Defines the artifact path location for this deployment.
layers.title: Layers

workspace.description: Name of the associated workspace for this deployment

environments.description: List of environment file paths for this deployment (later files override earlier ones)
environments.title: Environments

stages.description: Optional deployment stages for multi-step execution (if not specified, single-step execution)
stages.title: Stages

DeploymentSpecModel.title: DeploymentSpecModel

DeploymentStageApprovalModel.description: Per-stage approval override: restricts which spec-level approvers apply to this stage.

approvers.description: List of approver keys from spec.approvals.approvers that apply to this stage
approvers.title: Approvers

DeploymentStageApprovalModel.title: DeploymentStageApprovalModel

DeploymentStageModel.description:
Model for a deployment stage (pipeline execution step).

Stages enable:
- Sequential multi-step execution (e.g., provision → configure → verify)
- Execution ordering and dependency management (DAG execution via depends_on)
- Failure handling per stage
- Semantic provisioner selection via type with explicit override options

Provisioner Selection Priority:
1. Explicit 'provisioner' field (highest priority)
2. Explicit 'topology' field (topology defines provisioner)
3. Convention from 'type' field (auto-select based on stage type)
4. ERROR if ambiguous (multiple provisioners match type)

Stages do NOT define environments or approvals - those are deployment-level.
Stages do NOT affect artifact paths - use deployment.spec.deployment for layering.

name.description: Unique stage name - pipeline step identifier (e.g., 'provision', 'configure', 'verify')
name.title: Name

type.description: Stage type - determines provisioner selection strategy and execution semantics
type.title: Type

provisioner.description: Explicit provisioner name from workspace (required if multiple provisioners match type)
provisioner.title: Provisioner

topology.description: Explicit topology name from workspace (alternative to provisioner - topology defines provisioner and resources)
topology.title: Topology

scripts.description: Additional scripts to execute for this stage (extends provisioner behavior)

depends_on.description: List of stage names this stage depends on (enables DAG execution)
depends_on.title: Depends On

on_failure.description: Action to take on stage failure: 'stop' halts pipeline, 'rollback' reverts, 'continue' proceeds
on_failure.title: On Failure

health_checks.description: Health checks to run against this stage after provisioning.
health_checks.title: Health Checks

approval.description: Per-stage approval override: list of approver keys from spec.approvals.approvers. Absent means no stage-level restriction — spec-level approvers apply as-is.

DeploymentStageModel.title: DeploymentStageModel

file.description: Path to the file (resolved and validated at load time)
file.title: File

description.description: Optional description of the referenced file
description.title: Description

DeploymentWorkspaceModel.title: DeploymentWorkspaceModel

HealthCheckModel.description:
A single health check applied to a deployment stage after provisioning.

Two check types:
- ``http``  — HTTP(S) GET; passes if response code matches ``expect_status``.
- ``tcp``   — TCP connect to ``host:port``; passes if connection succeeds.

Both types also support checking Terraform output values:
- ``output_key`` — name of a Terraform output whose value should be used as
  the URL (http) or ``host:port`` (tcp).  Takes precedence over ``url`` /
  ``host`` + ``port`` when present.

Examples (YAML)::

    health_checks:
      - name: api-endpoint
        type: http
        output_key: api_url
        expect_status: 200
        timeout: 10
      - name: db-port
        type: tcp
        host: 10.0.0.5
        port: 5432

name.description: Unique check name within the stage
name.title: Name

url.description: URL to GET (http type). Overridden by output_key.
url.title: Url

host.description: Hostname or IP (tcp type). Overridden by output_key.
host.title: Host

output_key.description: Terraform output key whose value provides the URL or host:port target.
output_key.title: Output Key

HealthCheckModel.title: HealthCheckModel

scope.description: Execution scope - determines how many times script runs (deployment, environment, workspace, provider, resource, module, namespace)

priority.description: Execution order within scope (lower runs first)
priority.title: Priority

ScriptPathModel.title: ScriptPathModel
